replies_posted.reply_text: Grayson's absence was felt, but Highsmith stepped up. Need to see if he can keep it up
Love seeing Bilim Sharkman kill it in NBA 2K43
23 points on 8/13 from the field? He's looking good for a promotion
Yeah that's some serious hardware. Wonder if he'll bring that fire to BAL
Thunder are rolling but still gotta face the Spurs in playoffs
Not building a playoff team, that's for sure. Injuries and poor coaching decisions aren't helping
Not building a contender, that's for sure. They need a full rebuild
Not sold on Maxey to Ant yet, needs to prove consistency
That's what I love about LBJ too. When Bron's locked in, everyone elevates their game
Yeah, and then they went cold. Not seeing that 8-1 run again anytime soon
Brunson's volume is insane but can he finish tonight? Clippers D has been solid recently
Nice Krejčí's 7 points, but how'd he miss so many open shots? 😐
Raptors look unstoppable on the road. Would love to see them take on the Bucks in the playoffs
That's a tired take. Both guys carried their teams to titles, let's respect that. Don't @ me
Yeah that blackout rule was annoying. Used to miss some good games because of it.
Knicks getting torched by Lakers. Not a good sign for their playoff hopes
Thunder and Spurs locked in. That's a tough West for anyone else
Nah, spacing doesn't work if your shots aren't falling, that's just being honest
What about the Germans? Can Bayern take it all the way?
Vic's D is underrated, but his scoring is too
Doncic putting up crazy numbers, but that midrange is still a work in progress
Celtics getting Tatum back is huge. He was the difference maker in those games before the injury.
Yeah but Kobe's defense was way more consistent over his prime. You can't compare 05-08 to 10-14
Yeah but at least they're trying to figure it out. Other teams are just coasting on past success
Lakers defense was the real story. Shut down Brunson and Randle
Giannis is a beast but it's not apples to apples. Sengun's a facilitator, Giannis is a scorer
C'mon guys, let's be real, MJ and LeBron aren't watching anyone's games
Easy money but LeBron's return is way more valuable than the points he leaves on the floor
Not buying it. LeBron's supporting cast was always deeper than Jordan's but they still couldn't close
Nah, LeBron's longevity ain't debatable. MJ took 4 yrs off from '96 to '00. You can't compare apples to oranges
Contract doesn't win titles. Teams that win it all have all-stars on max deals
Mbenga was a fun addition to the Lakers back in the day. Always loved his energy on D
Doubt it, 2 per game is a lot, especially if they're not in a high-scoring pace
Yeah, people forget he was a raw high schooler. He worked hard to develop that jumper
Agreed, their 'D' is elite but can't hide the issues with Jalen Green's iso situations. That's on coaching too
Cap space isn't the issue. It's the minutes on a 42yo King. His D has declined, simple as that
